n_a_brilliant.ghostReplyPreview: Initech accepts the carve-out language if breach notification stays at 24h and Ontario-only residency is non-negotiable. Counter likely focuses on insurance minimums.

move.label: Concede SLA + audit cadence; hold a $5M aggregate cap with a $3M Section 9 sub-cap; accept Net 60
move.summary: Trade what we can survive (24/7 SLA tier, audit cadence, payment timing) for the only existential ask we can't take: uncapped breach liability.
move.riskNote: Initech will counter on insurance and may push the sub-cap up to $5M; pre-negotiate the ceiling with our broker.

n_b_risky.ghostReplyPreview: Initech will route to GC review and almost certainly pause the deal for 2-3 weeks. They have walked from comparable vendors at this stage.

move.label: Reject Section 9 uncapped carve-out outright; counter every enterprise ask with vendor-friendly language
move.summary: Hardball: defend the existential risk allocation but signal we may be unwilling to do business with regulated FIs at all.
move.riskNote: High potential value preserved if it lands; high probability the deal dies in committee.

n_c_blunder.ghostReplyPreview: Initech accepts in 24 hours, signs within the week. The deal closes — and lights a slow fuse on a balance-sheet event.

move.label: Accept Initech's full markup as drafted
move.summary: Sign the redlined MSA exactly as Initech sent it.
move.riskNote: One Section 9 incident wipes the company — uncapped liability + step-in + 24/7 ops without staffing.
